title: AOT/-r run-cache ships STALE output: cache key ignored (load …)ed / (require …)d dependency edits
goal: Reported by the noesis resident build (BUGS-2026-07-04 item #5, related #3): recompiling produced an object/binary that did not reflect recent source edits (exit 0, fresh mtime), consistent with 'an incremental cache keyed by something that doesn't invalidate on these edits'. Root cause: the persistent run-cache key (makeJitRunCacheKey in exe/eshkol-run.cpp) hashed ONLY the entry file's bytes, so editing a transitively (load "…")ed / (require …)d / (import …)ed dependency left the key unchanged and `eshkol-run -r` silently re-executed a STALE cached binary. noesis src/cli/main.esk (load …)s ~230 faculty files, so any edit to a loaded faculty produced stale runtime behavior ('changed function body still emits its OLD form'). The AOT (-o/--emit-object) path itself is uncached and atomic (writes temp + rename, hard-fails on error) — verified it faithfully emits fresh edits — so item #5's specific -o symptom is a build-system (ninja DEPENDS/CONFIGURE_DEPENDS, item #3, in the noesis repo) staleness; the reproducible eshkol-side cache-invalidation defect is the run-cache dependency-hash gap fixed here.
acceptance: editing a transitively (load …)ed dependency changes the run-cache key so `-r` MISSES and re-runs fresh code (no stale output)
an unchanged re-run still HITS the cache (the fix must not disable caching)
nested (2+ level) dependency edits also invalidate
the AOT -o path reflects a fresh edit (new top-level-reachable string present in the recompiled object)
existing entry-file invalidation and SICP 88/88 still pass
campaign_payoff: Silently shipping stale code is the worst build bug; closes a HIGH-severity stale-output hole for every -r program that loads/requires dependencies.
repro:
D=$(mktemp -d); printf '(define (m) "ONE")
' > $D/dep.esk; printf '(load "dep.esk")
(display (m))(newline)
' > $D/entry.esk; ESHKOL_JIT_CACHE_DIR=$D/c ESHKOL_JIT_CACHE_TRACE=1 build/eshkol-run -r $D/entry.esk; printf '(define (m) "TWO")
' > $D/dep.esk; ESHKOL_JIT_CACHE_DIR=$D/c ESHKOL_JIT_CACHE_TRACE=1 build/eshkol-run -r $D/entry.esk  # pre-fix: cache hit, prints ONE (STALE); post-fix: miss, prints TWO
